title: Miconia sulfurea
taxonomy: Miconia sulfurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Miconia, which comprises over 1,000 species of plants. The species Miconia sulfurea is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Miconia sulfurea is classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree. It is often found growing in humid, subtropical environments.

intro: Miconia sulfurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and conditions.
